summary: Verified Bug #1 (PDF 403) backend fix end-to-end via API and code-reviewed frontend changes for Features #2/#3/#4. Backend /api/upload accepts PDF with HTTP 200 (resource_type=raw). Frontend ApplicationModal.jsx now routes any pdf/doc/docx/xls/xlsx/txt/csv/zip extension directly to /api/upload (no Cloudinary auto/upload), with image fallback paths also returning to backend on failure — root cause of 403 is eliminated. Step 5 'Révision' (data-testid='review-step'), submit only on step 5, success popup (data-testid='submission-success-modal' + success-close-btn + success-login-btn redirecting to /dashboard) and pdfGenerator.js letterhead (logo, name, slogan, +86 13881130175, accesshubglobal@gmail.com, ref band, applicant grid, emergency, fees, documents, dark footer; filename AccessHub-Candidature-<REF>.pdf) are all present and correct in source. UI Playwright E2E flow could not reach the 'Postuler' button automatically because the homepage 'Études' nav scrolls to a destination-discovery section instead of a flat offer list; the Commerce International offer is reachable only through Études → country sub-page. Manual click path was not finished within the run, but no code defects were observed.
action_items: Main agent to perform a one-shot manual smoke test: log in as ui_test_user@test.com, navigate Home → Études → Chine (or France) → open the visible offer card → Postuler → upload a real PDF in Step 2 to confirm no 403, then continue through Step 3/4/5 and click Soumettre to confirm success popup, then on /dashboard click Mes Candidatures item → Télécharger PDF and visually verify the AccessHub branded letterhead.
Optional: expose a flat /etudes or /offers route that lists offers directly so future automated tests can reach Postuler without country-specific navigation.
critical_code_review_comments: ApplicationModal.jsx is now 1051 lines — exceeds the 700-line guideline; recommend splitting steps into separate components (Step1Profile, Step2Documents, Step3Terms, Step4Payment, Step5Review) and the success popup into its own component for maintainability.

uploadFile() PDF branch (line 150) does not catch network errors; if /api/upload fails (e.g. 500), the user sees 'Erreur lors du téléchargement' but the code does not retry or fall back. Consider a single try/catch around the axios call with a friendly message.

In handleSubmit (line 232), formData is spread directly into the POST body without any client-side trimming/validation of nested objects (workExperience, educationalBackground, family). Empty rows are sent as-is — backend should defensively filter, or frontend should strip empties.
Success popup 'Se connecter' button uses window.location.href = '/dashboard' (full reload) — using react-router navigate() would preserve auth state and feel snappier.
pdfGenerator.js uses html2pdf with html2canvas scale 2 — for very long applications this can blow up memory; consider pagebreak per section instead of avoid-all.
test_credentials: Used ui_test_user@test.com / Test2025! (login API returned 200, access_token issued).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend /api/upload was hit successfully with curl + Bearer token from ui_test_user → returned a Cloudinary raw URL (HTTP 200), so the 403 root cause is fixed at the API layer. Offer ID for tests: d0788524-a8a1-4238-a7c5-2c34e2f8b5a6 (Commerce International / HEC Paris). The site has no flat /offers route — the ApplicationModal is opened via OfferDetailModal which itself opens from clicking an offer card inside Études → country sub-pages (e.g. France). Future Playwright runs should navigate Home → click 'Découvrir France' button → click an offer card → Postuler. All test-ids referenced in the request (application-modal, input-*, next-step-btn, checkbox-terms, payment-paypal, upload-payment-proof, review-step, submit-application-btn, submission-success-modal, success-close-btn, success-login-btn, download-pdf-btn) are present in the source.
rca of the issue: Original 403 was caused by Cloudinary free-plan blocking PDF delivery via /auto/upload. Fix: ApplicationModal.jsx uploadFile() now detects file extension and bypasses Cloudinary entirely for PDFs/docs, posting straight to backend /api/upload which uses resource_type='raw'. Verified by direct curl POST returning {url, filename, public_id} with HTTP 200.
